title: excoecaria cuspidata
intro: Excoecaria cuspidata, also known as the "Yellow Oleander", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia and Australia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and shiny leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. Excoecaria cuspidata has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infused tea, and it is also used in traditional remedies to treat various ailments, including respiratory infections and digestive issues.
